mission.title: longhorn: [FEATURE] Pause IO when raid1 bdev snapshotting
mission.description: [FEATURE] Pause IO when raid1 bdev snapshotting. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current longhorn deployment
steps.description:
Verify your longhorn version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
helm list -n longhorn 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working longhorn installation.

steps.title: Review longhorn configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant longhorn configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
kubectl get configmap -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=longhorn
```
## Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe (👍 if you like this request)

To ensure each volume snapshot consistent among the replicas of each longhorn bdev/volume, need to implement a mechanism to pause IO of the bdev and

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n longhorn -l app.kubernetes.io/name=longhorn
kubectl get events -n longhorn --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[FEATURE] Pause IO when raid1 bdev snapshotting" is functioning correctly.
